bucket add:
Usage: add <bucket_name> [<repo>]
Add Scoop bucket
e.g.   scoop bucket add extras
bucket rm:
Usage: rm <bucket_name>
✨Remove Scoop bucket
e.g.   scoop bucket rm extras
bucket known:
List all official known buckets
When adding these buckets, you can add repo remote address
e.g.   scoop bucket add extras
search:
Usage: search <query>
Search installable applications
e.g.   scoop search sudo         --- Search sudo in each bucket (order follows scoop bucket list)
       scoop search main/sudo    --- Search sudo only in main
install:
Usage: install [options] <app>
✨✨Install application
e.g.   scoop install sudo         --- Search sudo in each bucket and install if found
       scoop install main/sudo    --- Search sudo only in main and install if found
       scoop install main/sudo -u --- Install application without updating Scoop
uninstall:
Usage: uninstall [options] <app>
✨Uninstall application
e.g.   scoop uninstall sudo
update:
Usage: update [options] [<app>]
✨Update application or Scoop itself
e.g.   scoop update       --- Update Scoop itself
       scoop update 7zip  --- Update 7zip
update -f: ✨Force update even if no new versions
list:
Usage: list [query]
List installed applications
e.g.   scoop list     --- List all installed applications
       scoop list i   --- List applications with names containing 'i'
       scoop list ^i  --- List applications starting with 'i'
       scoop list i$  --- List applications ending with 'i'
info:
Usage: info [--verbose] <app>
✨✨Display information about a specific application
e.g.   scoop info 7zip
cache:
Usage: cache [<show>|<rm>] [<app>] [<app>...]
✨Manage download cache
e.g.   scoop cache                --- Show all download caches
       scoop cache show 7zip      --- Show 7zip download cache
       scoop cache rm 7zip sudo   --- Remove 7zip sudo download cache
cache rm:
Remove download cache for specified applications
e.g.   scoop cache rm *     --- Remove all download caches
       scoop cache rm 7zip  --- Remove 7zip download cache
reset:
Usage: reset <app|*>
✨Reset applications to resolve conflicts
e.g.   scoop reset python
       scoop reset python27
cleanup:
Usage: cleanup [options] <app|*>
✨Clean up applications (Remove old versions)
e.g.   scoop cleanup 7zip
prefix:
Usage: prefix <app>
Return the path for a specified application
e.g.   scoop prefix 7zip
cat:
Usage: cat <app>
Display the content of a specified application's manifest
e.g.   scoop cat 7zip
checkup: Check if Scoop has potential issues
alias add:
Usage: add <alias_name> <command> <description>
Add command aliases
e.g.   scoop alias add upgrade 'scoop update *' 'Updates all apps'
       Type 'scoop upgrade' to run 'scoop update *'
alias rm:
Usage: rm <alias_name>
Remove command aliases
e.g.   scoop alias rm upgrade
shim rm: Remove shims (this may remove shims added by application manifests, be cautious)
shim list: List all shims or matching shims
shim alter: Replace the target source of a shim
config:
Usage: config [rm] name [value]
✨Manage Scoop configuration
e.g.   scoop config                 --- Get all configurations
       scoop config <name>          --- Get configuration
       scoop config <name> <value>  --- Set configuration
       scoop config rm <name>       --- Remove configuration
config use_external_7zip: ✨External 7zip (from path) will be used for archive extraction
config use_lessmsi: ✨Prioritize using the lessmsi utility over native msiexec
config no_junction: ✨'current' version aliases will not be used. Shims and shortcuts will point to specific versions
config scoop_repo: ✨Git repository containing Scoop source code. This configuration is for custom forks
config scoop_branch: ✨Allows using branches other than master, useful for testing specific features before being released to all users
If you want to receive updates in advance to test new features, use develop (Ref: https://github.com/ScoopInstaller/Scoop/issues/2939)
config proxy:
Usage: proxy [username:password@]host:port
By default, Scoop uses proxy settings from Internet Options but with anonymous authentication
To use the credentials of the current logged-in user, use 'currentuser' in place of username:password
To use the system's configured system proxy settings from Internet Options, use 'default' in place of host:port
An empty or unset value is equivalent to 'default' (no username or password)
To bypass the system proxy and connect directly, use 'none' (no username or password)
config autostash_on_conflict: ✨Scoop will automatically stash uncommitted changes when conflicts are detected during updates (default is false, which will abort the update)
config default_architecture: ✨Allows configuring the preferred application installation architecture.
If not specified, the system will automatically determine the architecture
config force_update: ✨Forces updating applications to the version in the bucket
config show_update_log: ✨Does not display 'scoop update' on change commits
config show_manifest: ✨Displays the manifest for each application about to be installed and asks the user whether to proceed
config global_path: Path to the root directory of global applications in Scoop
config cache_path: For downloads, defaults to the 'cache' folder under Scoop's root directory
config gh_token: GitHub API token used for authentication requests
Essential for running checkver and downloading from private repositories without hitting rate limits
config virustotal_api_key: API key for uploading/scanning files using virustotal
config cat_style:
When set to a non-empty string, Scoop will use 'bat' to display 'scoop cat' commands and manifests during manifest inspection
This requires installing 'bat' (run 'scoop install bat' to install it), otherwise, it will throw an error
Accepts values identical to those passed to the --style flag of 'bat'
config ignore_running_processes:
✨When set to false (default), Scoop will immediately stop its process if any target application processes are detected to be running
Processes here refer to reset/uninstall/update
When set to true, Scoop will only display a warning message and continue the process
config private_hosts: Array of private hosts requiring additional authentication
For e.g.   if you need to access a private GitHub repository, add the host to this list and provide matching and header strings
config hold_update_until:
Disable/hold Scoop self-updates until the specified date
'scoop hold scoop' sets the value to one day ahead
Should be provided in 'YYYY-MM-DD', 'YYYY/MM/DD', or any other format acceptable to '[System.DateTime]::Parse()'
Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.datetime.parse?view=netframework-4.5#StringToParse
config update_nightly: ✨Nightly versions are formatted as 'nightly-yyyyMMdd', if set to true, they will update one day ahead
Otherwise, nightly versions won't update unless --force is used
config aria2-enabled: ✨Whether to enable Aria2c for downloading artifacts
config aria2-warning-enabled: ✨Whether to disable warning messages displayed during Aria2c downloads
config aria2-split: Number of connections to use for downloading
config aria2-max-connection-per-server: Maximum connections per single server for each download
config aria2-min-split-size: Files to be split and downloaded using multiple connections based on the configured size
which:
Usage: which <command>
Locate a shim/executable (similar to the 'which' command on Linux)
e.g.   scoop which scoop
hold:
Usage: hold [-g] <app>
✨Disable updates for a specified app
e.g.   scoop hold 7zip
hold -g: Disable updates for a specified app globally
unhold:
Usage: unhold [-g] <app> 
✨Enable updates for a specified app
e.g.   scoop unhold 7zip
unhold -g: Enable updates for a specified app globally
export:
Usage: export [-c] > scoop_file.json
✨Export installed apps, buckets (and optional config) in JSON format
e.g.   scoop export -c > ~/Desktop/scoop_file.json   --- Export to scoop_file.json under the Desktop directory
export -c: Include Scoop's configuration in the export
import:
Usage: import <path|url>
Import installed apps, buckets (and config) from a specified file in JSON format
e.g.   scoop import ~/Desktop/scoop_file.json
depends:
Usage: depends <app>
List dependencies for an app, ordered by installation sequence
e.g.   scoop depends python
status:
Usage: status [-l]
✨Display status and check for new app versions
e.g.   scoop status
status -l: Check the status of locally installed apps only and disable remote fetching/checking of Scoop and buckets
download:
Usage: download [options] <app>
✨✨Download apps in the cache folder and verify hashes
e.g.   scoop download git                    --- Download an app without installing it
       scoop download gh@2.7.0               --- Download a different version of an app
       scoop download https://xxx/sudo.json  --- Download an app from a manifest via URL
       scoop download path/to/app.json       --- Download an app from a local manifest on the computer
download -u: ✨Only download without updating Scoop if there's an update available
download -f: ✨Force the download and overwrite the download cache
virustotal: ✨Look up the hash or URL of an app on virustotal.com
virustotal -u: ✨Only check without updating Scoop if there's an update available
virustotal -s:
Alias: --scan
✨Submit download URLs for analysis (and future retrieval) for packages without VirusTotal information
This requires configuring virustotal_api_key
virustotal -n: ✨By default, it also checks all dependencies. This option bypasses it
home:
Usage: home <app>
Open the homepage of an app
e.g.   scoop home sudo
-v: Show Scoop version and the latest commit information of the buckets
--version: Show Scoop version and the latest commit information of the buckets
